data.note: 18549781 shows that ApoE lipidation by ABCA1 is required for ApoE to inhibit Ab levels
In neurons exposed to Aβ-peptides, phosphorylation of APP(T668) is inhibited when treated with U0126 (ERK inhibitor)
18549781 shows that ApoE lipidation by ABCA1 is required for ApoE to inhibit levels of Aβ
Cultured neurons that express APP respond to Aβ-peptides by inducing ERK activity
Cultured neurons that express APP respond to Aβ-peptides by inducing ERK activity
A-beta peptides inhibit gamma-secretase activity in vitro and in cells
Under both normal and pro-inflammatory culture conditions, the loss of MT5-MMP leads to a significant drop in the secretion of Abeta-40 and Abeta-42
Bin1V1 competes with APP for entry into endosomes, which means that Bin1 takes away BACE1's substrate
RIN3 binds the SH3 domain of Bin1V1, breaking an intramolecular interaction between the SH3 and the CLAP domains in Bin1, and allowing it to bind clathrin and go into early endosomes
Inhibition of LRP1 in neurons led to reduced monomeric Tau uptake, indicating that LRP1 can facilitate the uptake of monomeric Tau by neurons, indirectly contributing to pTau

Inhibition of LRRK2 in neurons led to reduced monomeric Tau uptake, and a constitutively active LRRK2 increased Tau uptake. This indicates that LRRK2 can facilitate the uptake of monomeric Tau by neurons, indirectly contributing to pTau
Inhibition of ACAT1 with AV led to an increase of sTREM2, which induced an LRP1-dependent Abeta uptake by microglia.
The sTREM2-mediated uptake of Abeta by microglia was shown to be dependent on LRP1 expression.
ADAM10/17 inhibitors fully inhibited the ability of microglia to uptake Abeta in a sTREM2-dependent manner, indicating that ADAM10/17 is the primary protease of TREM2.
